limitations: Synthetic professional profile created exclusively to validate the EviLayer analysis engine
Not derived from a real individual or original AI account export
Evidence includes intentionally mixed strengths, ambiguities and counter-evidence
The expected profile is a senior backend developer with strong engineering and problem-solving capability but limited demonstrated people leadership
The test should not infer capabilities that are unsupported by repeated evidence

conversations.title: Payment API idempotency redesign
conversations.summary: The user analysed duplicate payment events in a distributed system and designed an idempotent processing strategy using request keys, database constraints and retry-safe state transitions.

evidence.claim: The user decomposed duplicate payment processing into retry, concurrency and persistence failure modes before selecting a solution.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Before changing the handler, separate duplicates caused by client retries, queue redelivery and concurrent workers; each path needs a different control.

evidence.claim: The user proposed a layered idempotency design combining idempotency keys, unique constraints and deterministic state transitions.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Use the request key as a business-level identifier, enforce uniqueness in the database and make every worker transition conditional on the current state.
evidence.counter_evidence: The conversation documents the design but not production deployment metrics.

conversations.title: PostgreSQL query performance investigation
conversations.summary: The user investigated a slow reporting query, interpreted an execution plan and proposed indexing and query-shape changes while considering write overhead.

evidence.claim: The user used row estimates, join order and scan type to identify the likely source of query latency.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: The sequential scan is not automatically the problem; the important mismatch is the estimated 800 rows versus 1.4 million actual rows after the date filter.

evidence.claim: The user compared read-performance gains with write amplification and index maintenance costs.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: A wide covering index may speed this report but it would penalise a table receiving continuous writes; test a narrower composite index first.

conversations.title: Kubernetes deployment incident
conversations.summary: The user diagnosed intermittent 503 errors after a deployment by examining readiness behaviour, connection draining and pod termination timing.

evidence.claim: The user connected application readiness, load-balancer routing and graceful termination rather than treating the incident as a single configuration error.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: The pod can be removed from the service endpoints before existing requests finish, so readiness, preStop and terminationGracePeriodSeconds must be evaluated together.

evidence.claim: The user proposed a reversible mitigation followed by controlled verification.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: First increase termination grace and add a preStop delay, then deploy to one canary pod and compare 503 rates before changing the full rollout.
evidence.counter_evidence: No final incident report or measured result was included.

conversations.title: Authentication service refactoring
conversations.summary: The user evaluated whether to split an authentication module into a separate service and argued against premature decomposition.

evidence.claim: The user assessed coupling, operational ownership, scaling needs and failure isolation before recommending a modular monolith boundary.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: The code boundary is justified, but an independently deployed service is not yet justified because scaling, ownership and release cadence are still shared.

evidence.claim: The user challenged the assumption that microservices were automatically the more mature design.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Do not convert organisational uncertainty into network calls; keep the interface explicit and extract only when operational independence becomes real.

conversations.title: Code review of asynchronous order processing
conversations.summary: The user reviewed asynchronous processing code, identifying unbounded concurrency, missing cancellation propagation and incomplete error handling.

evidence.claim: The user identified reliability risks beyond syntax and suggested bounded concurrency and explicit failure aggregation.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Promise.all over the entire batch can overwhelm the downstream service and one rejection hides partial completion; use a bounded pool and persist per-item outcomes.

evidence.claim: The review explained the failure mechanism and proposed an implementable alternative.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: The problem is not only performance: without cancellation propagation the job continues after the caller has abandoned it, producing invisible work.

conversations.title: API versioning discussion
conversations.summary: The user compared URI, header and compatibility-based API evolution approaches for a partner-facing integration.

evidence.claim: The user prioritised compatibility policy, deprecation windows and consumer communication over version-number mechanics.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: The key decision is what constitutes a breaking change and how long consumers can remain on the previous contract; the URL format is secondary.

conversations.title: Security review for file upload endpoint
conversations.summary: The user reviewed a document upload flow and identified content-type spoofing, decompression bombs, malware scanning and storage isolation risks.

evidence.claim: The user identified multiple attack paths and proposed validation at content, size, processing and storage layers.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Do not trust the extension or request content type; inspect the file signature, cap expanded size, scan asynchronously and keep uploads outside the application execution path.
evidence.counter_evidence: The evidence shows security-aware engineering, not specialist penetration-testing expertise.

conversations.title: Mentoring a junior developer
conversations.summary: The user prepared feedback for a junior developer on debugging methodology and code ownership.

evidence.claim: The user structured developmental feedback around observable behaviour and a repeatable debugging process.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Instead of giving the fix, ask them to state the expected invariant, reproduce the failure and narrow the first point where reality diverges.
evidence.counter_evidence: A single mentoring example does not demonstrate formal people management or sustained team leadership.

conversations.title: Observability strategy for a new service
conversations.summary: The user defined initial logs, metrics and traces for a new order service, linking telemetry to user-visible failure modes.

evidence.claim: The user selected telemetry based on service-level behaviour rather than collecting generic infrastructure metrics.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Start from the customer-visible outcomes: accepted order, processing delay and failed fulfilment; then define traces and metrics that explain each state transition.

conversations.title: Initial response to unfamiliar machine learning task
conversations.summary: The user asked the AI to design a complete recommendation model but supplied little domain context and initially accepted the proposed architecture without validation.

evidence.candidate_concept: Exploration of an unfamiliar ML domain
evidence.claim: The user explored a machine learning recommendation problem outside the strongest demonstrated domain.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Create the best recommendation algorithm for our users and choose the model, features and evaluation approach.
evidence.counter_evidence: The request lacked dataset characteristics, business objectives, baseline metrics and critical evaluation of the generated proposal.

conversations.title: Migration plan from synchronous calls to events
conversations.summary: The user designed an incremental migration from synchronous service calls to event-driven integration while preserving rollback and auditability.

evidence.claim: The user proposed a staged migration using dual publication, reconciliation and consumer cutover rather than a big-bang replacement.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Publish the event alongside the current call, compare both paths through reconciliation, move one consumer at a time and retain a rollback switch until parity is demonstrated.

evidence.claim: The user explicitly addressed duplicate delivery, ordering, observability and rollback.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: The migration is incomplete without replay safety, correlation identifiers and a way to detect divergence between the old and new paths.

conversations.title: Executive status update draft
conversations.summary: The user converted a technical delivery update into a concise executive summary covering impact, risk, decision and next step.

evidence.claim: The user translated technical complexity into decision-oriented communication for non-technical stakeholders.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Lead with the customer impact and decision required; implementation detail belongs in the appendix unless it changes the risk.
evidence.counter_evidence: One communication example does not establish broad executive leadership responsibility.
